title: v2ecoli vs Beulig 2025 batch-phase biomass-density gap (preliminary)
caption: Final biomass concentration (g/L) from existing mbp-02 multigen runs at cpa ∈ {1, 1e6, 1e9}, overlaid against Beulig 2025 batch-phase target band (50-80 g/L). Star marks the cpa that would close the residual gap at the current 2-gen / 60-sim-min batch duration: ~2e+13.
interpretation: cpa=1 (literal single-cell) reaches ~3.3e-12 g/L at t=2-gen. cpa=1e9 (Beulig-regime representative-sampling) reaches ~3.3e-03 g/L — still 4.2 orders below Beulig's stated 50-80 g/L target. The residual gap is NOT a cpa-scaling problem (cpa is exact, see chart 04 variance bands); it's a BATCH-DURATION problem. v2ecoli's current 60-sim-min / 2-gen runs are far short of Beulig's ~6 h batch phase. Closing the gap requires (a) longer batch duration to accumulate more cell mass per agent, (b) cpa ~ 2e+13 (near Avogadro-mol scale — likely impractical), OR (c) fed-batch operations (deferred upstream blocker, pbg-bioreactor-transport-fork). mbp-05's comparison harness can render this gap explicitly BEFORE evaluating whether v2ecoli's biology reproduces Beulig's trajectory — diagnostic-first, evaluative-second.
